problem.summary: Gather embedding rows for token ids. The baseline directly copies each requested row. The optimized scaffold assumes ids have been sorted and uniqued by an upstream rocPRIM/hipCUB/rocThrust-style path, gathers unique rows once, and scatters back through an inverse map.
constraints: optimized seed expects unique_ids and inverse to be precomputed
dim is runtime dynamic but contiguous
dedupe only helps when repeated ids amortize sorting and scatter overhead

verification.method: TODO: compare direct gather and dedupe path outputs bytewise for deterministic fp32 table values.

curation.notes: Template-only embedding gather/dedup scaffold. The optimized path documents the sorted/unique customization surface; it must include sort and inverse-map cost before claiming an end-to-end win.
